name: PageIndex
tagline: Vectorless, reasoning-based RAG that builds a tree index from long documents
seoTitle: PageIndex: Vectorless Reasoning-Based RAG for Long Docs
metaDescription: PageIndex is a vectorless, reasoning-based RAG system that builds a hierarchical tree index from long PDFs and retrieves sections via LLM tree search.
overview: PageIndex is an open-source RAG system that works without a vector database and without chunking. Instead of matching text by vector similarity, it builds a hierarchical tree index from a long document, much like a table of contents, and lets an LLM reason over that tree to find the sections that are actually relevant.

The approach is aimed at long, professional documents such as financial reports, regulatory filings, legal manuals, and academic textbooks that exceed an LLM's context window. Because retrieval is driven by reasoning and grounded in explicit page and section references, every result is traceable and easier to explain than opaque vector search.
You can self-host the project with this repository using standard PDF parsing, or use the hosted cloud service and API for enhanced OCR and tree building.
features: No vector database: retrieval uses document structure and LLM reasoning instead of vector similarity search
No chunking: documents are organized into natural sections rather than artificial fixed-size chunks
Builds a semantic tree (table-of-contents style) index from long PDFs, with node IDs, summaries, and page ranges
Traceable, explainable retrieval grounded in explicit page and section references
Context-aware retrieval that can take conversation history and domain knowledge into account
Markdown input support and multi-LLM support via LiteLLM

gettingStarted.intro: Self-host PageIndex to generate a tree structure from a PDF document. You need Python and an LLM API key.

steps.heading: Install dependencies
steps.body: Install the required Python packages from the repository's requirements file.

steps.heading: Set your LLM API key
steps.body: Create a .env file in the root directory with your LLM API key. Multiple LLM providers are supported through LiteLLM.

steps.heading: Generate a PageIndex tree for your PDF
steps.body: Run the script against your document to build its tree structure index. A --md_path flag is also available for Markdown files.

steps.heading: Try the agentic vectorless RAG demo
steps.body: Install the optional dependency and run the included end-to-end example, which uses self-hosted PageIndex with the OpenAI Agents SDK.

useCases: Question answering over long financial reports, SEC filings, and earnings disclosures
Reasoning-based retrieval across regulatory, legal, or technical manuals that exceed an LLM context window
Searching and extracting answers from academic textbooks and other complex long-form PDFs
Building agentic RAG pipelines where retrieval needs to be traceable and explainable instead of relying on vector similarity
